name: Blueberry-Lemon Muffins
recipeInstructions:
Glaze:
4 Tablespoons Butter
1/2 Cup Sugar
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. If using a convection oven, reduce heat by 40 degrees.
Sift the flour and add the other dry ingredients into a mixing bowl.
In a small bowl, crack the eggs and beat with a wire whisk for 10 seconds.
Add the milk and oil to the eggs and stir to blend.
Make a well in the flour mixture and pour in the egg-milk blend.
Stir as little as possible to moisten the flour. Set aside briefly.
Combine the blueberries with the lemon zest, then fold into the muffin mix.
Again, stir gently and as little as possible.
Spoon batter into tins 3/4 full (18 muffins in a medium tin or 36 in a small tin).
Set timer for 20 minutes. Check at 15 minutes; if browning too quickly, move tray to lower shelf.
Melt butter in a small saucepan and pour sugar into a bowl or pan.
Cool muffins for 5 minutes before handling.
Tilt the tray, gently lift each muffin, dip top lightly in melted butter, then in sugar.
Serve fresh or store overnight. They can also be frozen, but fresh baked is best.
